tournament.name: 2nd Chance PTQ - PT Amsterdam - Wizards of the Coast

decks.notes: This deck focuses on utilizing the power of Eldrazi creatures and artifacts to create a devastating combo that puts immense pressure on the opponent. By using cards like Basking Broodscale, Devourer of Destiny, and Glaring Fleshraker in combination with Blade of the Bloodchief and Malevolent Rumble, the deck aims to quickly overwhelm the opponent with a swarm of powerful creatures. Emrakul, the Promised End and Sowing Mycospawn provide additional threats, while cards like Kozilek's Command and Ancient Stirrings help facilitate the combo. Key lands like Eldrazi Temple and Ugin's Labyrinth provide mana acceleration and protection for the deck's strategy.

The deck focuses on using aggressive creatures and spells to quickly control the board, while also utilizing blink effects to generate value and disrupt the opponent's game plan. With a mix of removal, counterspells, and powerful creatures like Phelia, Exuberant Shepherd and Overlord of the Balemurk, the deck aims to overwhelm the opponent and maintain tempo advantage. The inclusion of cards like Teferi, Time Raveler and Consign to Memory provides additional flexibility and disruption, allowing the deck to adapt to different matchups and strategies.

The main strategy of this deck is to quickly generate mana and play Allosaurus Rider, which can be sacrificed to search for powerful creatures like Griselbrand or Ghalta, Stampede Tyrant. The deck also includes a variety of spells and creatures that help to generate mana and protect the combo pieces, allowing the player to assemble their combo and win the game quickly. The deck focuses on utilizing different combos and synergies to overwhelm the opponent and achieve victory in a Modern 1 vs 1 format.

This deck focuses on utilizing Ral, Monsoon Mage's abilities to generate extra mana and cast spells for free, combining with rituals and cantrips to storm off and finish the game with a large Grapeshot. The key strategy is to chain together spells, generating mana and card advantage to overwhelm the opponent in a single turn. The deck also includes disruptive elements like Prismatic Ending and Wish to handle opposing threats and protect its combo. Additionally, Valakut Awakening and Past in Flames provide graveyard recursion to keep the engine going.

This Boros Aggro deck focuses on efficiently playing aggressive creatures and using combat tricks to overwhelm the opponent quickly. The deck also includes some control elements like Goblin Bombardment and Blood Moon to disrupt the opponent's game plan. The main strategy is to establish an early board presence with low cost creatures like Ragavan, Nimble Pilferer and Ocelot Pride, while also utilizing cards like Ajani, Nacatl Pariah and Ranger-Captain of Eos for added value and card advantage. The deck aims to close out games swiftly before the opponent can stabilize.

The deck focuses on ramping up mana quickly to cast powerful Eldrazi creatures while controlling the game with removal spells and disruptive cards. The main goal is to overwhelm the opponent with massive threats like Emrakul, the Promised End and Ulamog, the Ceaseless Hunger, while using cards like Fight Rigging and Kozilek's Command to disrupt their strategy. The deck also utilizes cards like Green Sun's Zenith and Disciple of Freyalise to accelerate mana production and maintain board presence. Overall, it aims to outpace and outlast opponents with a combination of ramp, control, and powerful threats.

The deck focuses on utilizing aggressive creatures and abilities that allow for blink effects, allowing you to generate incremental advantage and disrupt your opponent's game plan. By constantly flickering your creatures like Overlord of the Balemurk and Phelia, Exuberant Shepherd, you can control the board and apply pressure while also utilizing cards like Thoughtseize to disrupt your opponent's hand. With efficient removal like Fatal Push and interaction like Teferi, Time Raveler, you can maintain tempo and outmaneuver your opponent in a Modern 1 vs 1 setting.

The deck focuses on utilizing artifacts and combo pieces to generate a powerful board state and control the game. By leveraging cards like Emry, Lurker of the Loch and Oswald Fiddlebender, it can tutor for key pieces like Grinding Station and Aether Spellbomb to set up a win condition. With access to cards like Metallic Rebuke and Mishra's Bauble for disruption, the deck aims to outvalue and outmaneuver opponents through efficient artifact synergy. The goal is to assemble a combo quickly and efficiently to overwhelm the opponent and secure a victory.

The deck focuses on utilizing creatures like Allosaurus Rider and Eldritch Evolution to quickly assemble powerful combos on the battlefield. The goal is to ramp up mana with cards like Disciple of Freyalise and Wistfulness, while also utilizing cards like Neoform and Consign to Memory to search for key pieces. The inclusion of cards like Pact of Negation and Veil of Summer helps protect the combo from disruption. Overall, the deck aims to quickly overwhelm the opponent with large creatures and game-winning combinations.

This Boros Aggro deck focuses on aggression and quick tempo plays, utilizing powerful creatures like Ajani, Guide of Souls, and Ragavan, Nimble Pilferer to pressure the opponent. Additional support comes from cards like Ocelot Pride and Seasoned Pyromancer to maintain board presence. The deck also includes removal spells like Galvanic Discharge and Thraben Charm to handle threats, with versatile options like Ranger-Captain of Eos for utility. Key cards like Solitude and Goblin Bombardment provide additional flexibility for different matchups. The deck aims to overwhelm the opponent with efficient creatures and disruptive spells to secure victory.

The main concept of this deck is to control the game by disrupting the opponent's strategy with cards like Solitude, Cleansing Wildfire, and Path to Exile. The deck focuses on mana denial with cards like Field of Ruin and Demolition Field, while also utilizing board wipes like Wrath of God and Wrath of the Skies to maintain control. The inclusion of Kaheera, the Orphanguard as a companion adds an extra element of strategy to the deck. Overall, this Boros Ponza deck aims to hinder the opponent's resources and maintain board control to secure victory.

The deck focuses on controlling the opponent's board through various removal spells and hand disruption. It also utilizes graveyard recursion with cards like Necrodominance and Sheoldred, the Apocalypse. The win condition is to outvalue the opponent with cards like Soul Spike and Professor Dellian Fel, while maintaining control through disruption and removal. The deck's strategy is to grind out the opponent by effectively controlling the game and overwhelming them with powerful threats and recursion.

This deck aims to quickly fill the graveyard with powerful creatures like Atraxa and Griselbrand, then use cards like Goryo's Vengeance and Ephemerate to bring them back into play for a surprise attack. By utilizing cards like Psychic Frog and Quantum Riddler to control the opponent's board and disrupt their strategy, this deck can set up its combo pieces while staying alive. The deck also includes a mix of removal spells, hand disruption, and counter spells to protect its game plan. By carefully managing resources and timing its plays, this deck can overwhelm the opponent with its powerful reanimation strategy.

The key concept of this deck is to swarm the battlefield with cheap artifacts and creatures, utilizing Urza's Saga to create additional threats and generate value. It aims to overwhelm the opponent quickly with an aggressive strategy, while also disrupting their game plan with cards like Engineered Explosives and Metallic Rebuke. The deck uses a combination of artifact synergy, card advantage, and disruption to maintain tempo and pressure the opponent, ultimately aiming to win the game before the opponent can establish their own strategy.

This deck focuses on utilizing low-cost creatures and spells to quickly deal damage to the opponent. It aims to overwhelm the opponent with a combination of efficient creatures like Monastery Swiftspear, Dragon's Rage Channeler, and Slickshot Show-Off, along with burn spells like Lightning Bolt and Lava Dart. The deck also includes cantrips and card selection spells like Expressive Iteration and Preordain to ensure a consistent flow of threats. The mana base is designed to support the deck's aggressive game plan with a mix of fetch lands and dual lands.

This deck focuses on fast and aggressive gameplay, utilizing creatures with low mana cost and spells that can quickly deal damage to opponents. By combining creatures like Monastery Swiftspear and Dragon's Rage Channeler with spells like Lightning Bolt and Mutagenic Growth, this deck aims to overwhelm the opponent with early aggression. The inclusion of cards like Slickshot Show-Off and Cori-Steel Cutter adds versatility and additional sources of damage. The goal is to establish board control quickly and finish off the opponent before they can stabilize.

This deck focuses on assembling the Allosaurus Rider combo, which involves casting it for free using cards like Neoform and Eldritch Evolution to search for powerful creatures like Griselbrand or Ghalta, Stampede Tyrant. The key is to generate enough mana quickly with cards like Planar Genesis and Disciple of Freyalise to combo off before the opponent can stop you. Veil of Summer and Pact of Negation help protect the combo while Consign to Memory can disrupt the opponent's plans. The goal is to overwhelm the opponent with a powerful creature and win the game in a single explosive turn.

This deck focuses on using Allosaurus Rider as a combo piece to ramp into powerful creatures like Ghalta, Stampede Tyrant and Griselbrand. It utilizes cards like Eldritch Evolution and Neoform to tutor for key pieces, while also running cards like Pact of Negation for protection. The deck also includes mana ramp with cards like Disciple of Freyalise and Planar Genesis, and includes utility cards like Nature's Claim for removal. Overall, the deck aims to quickly assemble its combo pieces to overwhelm the opponent and secure a victory.

The deck focuses on using aggressive creatures like Ragavan and Ocelot Pride to quickly overwhelm the opponent. It also utilizes powerful planeswalkers like Ajani and Boromir to provide additional threats and support for the creatures. The inclusion of cards like Goblin Bombardment and Galvanic Discharge adds reach and removal to the deck's game plan. The mana base is designed to support the deck's aggressive curve while also including utility lands like Arena of Glory and Dalkovan Encampment for additional value. The deck aims to win the game quickly through a combination of creature damage and direct burn spells.

The deck focuses on quickly overwhelming the opponent with a mix of aggressive creatures and burn spells. It utilizes efficient creatures like Ragavan, Nimble Pilferer and Guide of Souls to apply pressure early on in the game. Ajani, Nacatl Pariah and Ocelot Pride provide additional threats that can quickly close out the game. The deck also includes some disruption in the form of Ranger-Captain of Eos and removal spells like Galvanic Discharge and Solitude. Blood Moon and Static Prison are included to disrupt the opponent's mana base and slow down their game plan.

This Boros Aggro deck focuses on a fast-paced, aggressive playstyle with a mix of creatures and burn spells to apply pressure to the opponent quickly. The deck includes powerful cards like Ajani, Nacatl Pariah and Ragavan, Nimble Pilferer to dominate the board early on. With a mix of evasion, removal, and card advantage, this deck aims to overwhelm the opponent and finish the game swiftly before they have a chance to stabilize. By efficiently utilizing its cards and mana base, this deck can consistently deliver a strong offensive strategy.

The main focus of this Boros Ponza control deck is to disrupt opponents' mana bases while using efficient removal and board wipes to control the game. It maintains a proactive strategy with the ability to disrupt opponent's game plans through land destruction, removal spells, and board wipes. The deck also utilizes a companion creature to provide additional value and flexibility in certain matchups. By controlling the board and disrupting opponent's resources, this deck aims to establish dominance and outvalue opponents in the long game.

The deck focuses on utilizing the Broodscale Bloodchief combo to generate a powerful board presence. By playing Basking Broodscale and Writhing Chrysalis early on, the deck can ramp up and quickly bring out powerful creatures like Emrakul, the Promised End and Glaring Fleshraker. With support cards like Blade of the Bloodchief, Kozilek's Command, and Malevolent Rumble, the deck aims to control the board while setting up its combo pieces. Urza's Saga, Ancient Stirrings, and Eldrazi Temple provide additional consistency and ramp, making the deck a potent force in the Modern format.

This deck focuses on ramping up mana quickly to cast powerful Eldrazi creatures while controlling the opponent's board with spells like Kozilek's Command and Malevolent Rumble. The deck utilizes cards like Green Sun's Zenith to search for key creatures like Emrakul, the Promised End and Ulamog, the Ceaseless Hunger. Fanatic of Rhonas provides additional ramp, while Disciple of Freyalise helps generate mana. Slumbering Trudge can also act as a solid blocker. The goal is to overwhelm the opponent with massive Eldrazi creatures while maintaining control over the game.

This deck focuses on using Eldrazi creatures to ramp up mana and control the game with various spells and abilities. The key strategy is to quickly gain access to powerful Eldrazi creatures like Emrakul, the Promised End, and Ugin, Eye of the Storms, while disrupting opponents with cards like Kozilek's Command and Fight Rigging. The deck also utilizes ramp spells like Green Sun's Zenith and Turntimber Symbiosis to accelerate mana production. By combining ramp, control, and powerful threats, the deck aims to overwhelm opponents and dominate the game.

The deck relies on using Atraxa, Grand Unifier and other creatures like Psychic Frog and Quantum Riddler to quickly fill the graveyard with powerful creatures, then using Goryo's Vengeance to bring them back into play for a surprise combo win. Cards like Solitude, Ephemerate, and Faithful Mending help protect and dig for combo pieces. Thoughtseize and Force of Negation provide disruption against opponents. The deck also includes a small control package with Prismatic Ending and Wrath of the Skies to deal with threats. The land base is designed to provide various colored mana sources for versatility.

This deck focuses on utilizing instant spells to reanimate powerful creatures like Griselbrand and Superior Spider-Man quickly and efficiently. The key strategy involves using cards like Ephemerate and Goryo's Vengeance to bring back creatures from the graveyard, while also disrupting opponents with cards like Thoughtseize and Force of Negation. The deck aims to assemble the combo pieces needed to reanimate big threats while also controlling the board and disrupting the opponent's game plan. It seeks to win the game through powerful reanimated creatures and clever spell interactions.

This deck focuses on quickly filling the graveyard with creatures, then using cards like Goryo's Vengeance and Ephemerate to bring them back into play for a powerful combo. Atraxa, Grand Unifier serves as a central piece to the strategy, providing additional value to the reanimated creatures. Cards like Solitude and Psychic Frog help control the board and disrupt the opponent's game plan. With a mix of reanimation spells, disruption, and creatures with powerful abilities, this deck aims to overwhelm the opponent with a quick and efficient combo strategy.

The deck utilizes creatures with the Cascade ability to cast Living End, a powerful sorcery that cycles all creatures from both players' graveyards back onto the battlefield. By manipulating cards in the graveyard and using counterspells like Force of Negation to protect their combo, the player aims to overwhelm their opponent with a board full of powerful creatures while disrupting their game plan. The deck also includes cards like Deceit and Endurance to control the game and outvalue the opponent. With skilled resource management and timing, the player can effectively execute their combo and secure victory.

This deck focuses on quickly generating and sacrificing artifacts to fuel powerful synergies and overwhelm the opponent with a fast and aggressive strategy. By utilizing cards like Kappa Cannoneer and Pinnacle Emissary, along with various artifact-based spells and abilities, the deck aims to put pressure on the opponent early in the game and maintain a strong board presence. The inclusion of cards like Engineered Explosives and Tormod's Crypt also provide utility and disruption options to control the game and ensure victory through efficient plays and resource management.

This UR Aggro deck focuses on quickly developing a board presence with efficient creatures like Dragon's Rage Channeler and Monastery Swiftspear, supported by burn spells like Lightning Bolt and Lava Dart to remove threats and finish off opponents. Mishra's Bauble allows for extra card draw and synergy with Dragon's Rage Channeler. The deck aims to aggressively attack the opponent's life total with a mix of creatures and burn spells, capitalizing on tempo plays and leveraging Prowess triggers to quickly close out games.

This deck focuses on fast-paced, aggressive plays using low-cost creatures and burn spells to quickly deal damage to the opponent. It aims to overwhelm the opponent with a combination of creatures like Dragon's Rage Channeler, Monastery Swiftspear, and Slickshot Show-Off, along with spells like Lightning Bolt and Lava Dart. The deck also includes pump spells like Mutagenic Growth and Monstrous Rage to increase the damage output. With a mana base that allows for quick access to the necessary colors, this deck aims to finish the game swiftly before the opponent can establish a strong defense.
